title: Stop the Bleed
subtitle: Sequence hemorrhage-control escalation for the wound in front of you.

cases.prompt: A 9-year-old has a deep forearm laceration from broken glass with brisk dark-red bleeding that soaks through gauze.
cases.hint: Start simple, then escalate when pressure is failing. Extremity wounds can receive a tourniquet when bleeding is uncontrolled.

steps.label: Apply tourniquet proximal to wound if uncontrolled
Reassess bleeding, distal perfusion, and shock signs

cases.explanation: For an extremity wound, begin with exposure and direct pressure. If bleeding remains uncontrolled, escalate to pressure/hemostatic dressing and tourniquet, then reassess perfusion and shock during transport.

cases.prompt: A child fell onto a metal fence and has a deep groin wound with ongoing heavy bleeding.
cases.hint: A junctional wound cannot be controlled by a standard limb tourniquet. Think packing and continuous pressure.
cases.explanation: Junctional hemorrhage requires packing and sustained pressure. A standard limb tourniquet is not the primary control for a groin wound.

cases.prompt: A teenager has a chest stab wound with bubbling blood and increasing respiratory distress.
cases.hint: Torso bleeding is not managed with a limb tourniquet. Seal the chest and reassess breathing.

steps.label: Reassess breathing and signs of tension physiology

cases.explanation: A sucking chest wound needs a chest seal and frequent respiratory reassessment. Tourniquets and wound packing are not the correct primary intervention for this torso wound.

cases.prompt: A 6-year-old has traumatic partial amputation of the lower leg from farm equipment with pulsatile bleeding.
cases.hint: Life-threatening extremity bleeding can justify early tourniquet use after immediate exposure/pressure.
cases.explanation: Massive extremity hemorrhage may require rapid tourniquet placement. After placement, document time and continuously reassess bleeding and shock.

cases.prompt: A child has scalp bleeding after a fall. The wound is oozing steadily, but bleeding slows with firm pressure.
cases.hint: If pressure is working, do not jump to interventions designed for uncontrolled extremity hemorrhage.

steps.label: Transport based on mechanism and findings

cases.explanation: Controlled scalp bleeding calls for direct pressure and pressure dressing while reassessing neuro status and mechanism. A tourniquet is not appropriate.
